petitioner_background: The petitioner is a synchronized swimming athlete, coach, and performer from Venezuela who sought classification as an alien of extraordinary ability under EB-1A (INA § 203(b)(1)(A)). She was a member of the Venezuelan national team, representing her country at multiple national and international competitions, participated in several Olympic Games qualifiers and national championships, and was recognized on multiple occasions as the regional-level athlete of the year. Her involvement in synchronized swimming spans more than two decades, having started at age five. She last entered the United States in 2016 when she and her family left Venezuela.
outcome_reasoning: The AAO dismissed the appeal based on the final merits determination, concluding that the petitioner failed to demonstrate sustained national or international acclaim by a preponderance of the evidence. Although the Director found she met four regulatory criteria, the AAO agreed with the Director that her acclaim abruptly ended around 2015-2016 and was nonexistent for seven or eight years through the 2023 petition filing. The AAO rejected her explanations: job offers to coach national teams from 2017-2020 did not equate to the required acclaim, her COVID-19 pandemic explanation failed to address the post-pandemic period after Florida reopened, and her recent joining of a professional synchronized swimming and water dance company occurred post-petition filing and lacked documented accolades. The AAO concluded that for someone with two decades in the sport, a seven-to-eight-year hiatus indicated a failure to sustain acclaim at a commensurate level, and her achievements did not rise to a 'career of acclaimed work in the field' as contemplated by Congress. The AAO reserved the remaining appellate arguments, including issues around her transition from athlete to coach, as the sustained acclaim determination was dispositive.
legal_citations: Matter of Christo's Inc., 26 I&N Dec. 537, 537 n.2 (AAO 2015)
Matter of Chen, 28 I&N Dec. 676, 677 n.1, 678 (BIA 2023)
Matter of Price, 20 I&N Dec. 953, 954 (Assoc. Comm'r 1994)
